function.name: memory
function.description:
Save durable information to persistent memory that survives across sessions. Memory is injected into future turns, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.

WHEN TO SAVE (do this proactively, don't wait to be asked):
- User corrects you or says 'remember this' / 'don't do that again'
- User shares a preference, habit, or personal detail (name, role, timezone, coding style)
- You discover something about the environment (OS, installed tools, project structure)
- You learn a convention, API quirk, or workflow specific to this user's setup
- You identify a stable fact that will be useful again in future sessions

PRIORITY: User preferences and corrections > environment facts > procedural knowledge. The most valuable memory prevents the user from having to repeat themselves.

Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts.
If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.

TWO TARGETS:
- 'user': who the user is -- name, role, preferences, communication style, pet peeves
- 'memory': your notes -- environment facts, project conventions, tool quirks, lessons learned

ACTIONS: add (new entry), replace (update existing -- old_text identifies it), remove (delete -- old_text identifies it).

SKIP: trivial/obvious info, things easily re-discovered, raw data dumps, and temporary task state.

target.description: Which memory store: 'memory' for personal notes, 'user' for user profile.

content.description: The entry content. Required for 'add' and 'replace'.

old_text.description: Short unique substring identifying the entry to replace or remove.

function.name: skill_manage
function.description:
Manage skills (create, update, delete). Skills are your procedural memory — reusable approaches for recurring task types. New skills go to ~/.hermes/skills/; existing skills can be modified wherever they live.

Actions: create (full SKILL.md + optional category), patch (old_string/new_string — preferred for fixes), edit (full SKILL.md rewrite — major overhauls only), delete, write_file, remove_file.

On delete, pass `absorbed_into=<umbrella>` when you're merging this skill's content into another one, or `absorbed_into=""` when you're pruning it with no forwarding target. This lets the curator tell consolidation from pruning without guessing, so downstream consumers (cron jobs that reference the old skill name, etc.) get updated correctly. The target you name in `absorbed_into` must already exist — create/patch the umbrella first, then delete.

Create when: complex task succeeded (5+ calls), errors overcome, user-corrected approach worked, non-trivial workflow discovered, or user asks you to remember a procedure.
Update when: instructions stale/wrong, OS-specific failures, missing steps or pitfalls found during use. If you used a skill and hit issues not covered by it, patch it immediately.

After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. Skip for simple one-offs. Confirm with user before creating/deleting.

Good skills: trigger conditions, numbered steps with exact commands, pitfalls section, verification steps. Use skill_view() to see format examples.

Pinned skills are protected from deletion only — skill_manage(action='delete') will refuse with a message pointing the user to `hermes curator unpin <name>`. Patches and edits go through on pinned skills so you can still improve them as pitfalls come up; pin only guards against irrecoverable loss.

name.description: Skill name (lowercase, hyphens/underscores, max 64 chars). Must match an existing skill for patch/edit/delete/write_file/remove_file.

content.description: Full SKILL.md content (YAML frontmatter + markdown body). Required for 'create' and 'edit'. For 'edit', read the skill first with skill_view() and provide the complete updated text.

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'patch'). Must be unique unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'patch'). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: For 'patch': replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false).

category.description: Optional category/domain for organizing the skill (e.g., 'devops', 'data-science', 'mlops'). Creates a subdirectory grouping. Only used with 'create'.

file_path.description: Path to a supporting file within the skill directory. For 'write_file'/'remove_file': required, must be under references/, templates/, scripts/, or assets/. For 'patch': optional, defaults to SKILL.md if omitted.

file_content.description: Content for the file. Required for 'write_file'.

absorbed_into.description: For 'delete' only — declares intent so the curator can tell consolidation from pruning without guessing. Pass the umbrella skill name when this skill's content was merged into another (the target must already exist). Pass an empty string when the skill is truly stale and being pruned with no forwarding target. Omitting the arg on delete is supported for backward compatibility but downstream tooling (e.g. cron-job skill reference rewriting) will have to guess at intent.

# Obsidian Flavored Markdown Skill

Create and edit valid Obsidian Flavored Markdown. Obsidian extends CommonMark and GFM with wikilinks, embeds, callouts, properties, comments, and other syntax. This skill covers only Obsidian-specific extensions -- standard Markdown (headings, bold, italic, lists, quotes, code blocks, tables) is assumed knowledge.

## Workflow: Creating an Obsidian Note

1. **Add frontmatter** with properties (title, tags, aliases) at the top of the file. See [PROPERTIES.md](references/PROPERTIES.md) for all property types.
2. **Write content** using standard Markdown for structure, plus Obsidian-specific syntax below.
3. **Link related notes** using wikilinks (`[[Note]]`) for internal vault connections, or standard Markdown links for external URLs.
4. **Embed content** from other notes, images, or PDFs using the `![[embed]]` syntax. See [EMBEDS.md](references/EMBEDS.md) for all embed types.
5. **Add callouts** for highlighted information using `> [!type]` syntax. See [CALLOUTS.md](references/CALLOUTS.md) for all callout types.
6. **Verify** the note renders correctly in Obsidian's reading view.

> When choosing between wikilinks and Markdown links: use `[[wikilinks]]` for notes within the vault (Obsidian tracks renames automatically) and `[text](url)` for external URLs only.

## Internal Links (Wikilinks)

```markdown
[[Note Name]]                          Link to note
[[Note Name|Display Text]]             Custom display text
[[Note Name#Heading]]                  Link to heading
[[Note Name#^block-id]]                Link to block
[[#Heading in same note]]              Same-note heading link
```

Define a block ID by appending `^block-id` to any paragraph:

```markdown
This paragraph can be linked to. ^my-block-id
```

For lists and quotes, place the block ID on a separate line after the block:

```markdown
> A quote block

^quote-id
```

## Embeds

Prefix any wikilink with `!` to embed its content inline:

```markdown
![[Note Name]]                         Embed full note
![[Note Name#Heading]]                 Embed section
![[image.png]]                         Embed image
![[image.png|300]]                     Embed image with width
![[document.pdf#page=3]]               Embed PDF page
```

See [EMBEDS.md](references/EMBEDS.md) for audio, video, search embeds, and external images.

## Callouts

```markdown
> [!note]
> Basic callout.

> [!warning] Custom Title
> Callout with a custom title.

> [!faq]- Collapsed by default
> Foldable callout (- collapsed, + expanded).
```

Common types: `note`, `tip`, `warning`, `info`, `example`, `quote`, `bug`, `danger`, `success`, `failure`, `question`, `abstract`, `todo`.

See [CALLOUTS.md](references/CALLOUTS.md) for the full list with aliases, nesting, and custom CSS callouts.

## Properties (Frontmatter)

```yaml
---
title: My Note
date: 2024-01-15
tags:
  - project
  - active
aliases:
  - Alternative Name
cssclasses:
  - custom-class
---
```

Default properties: `tags` (searchable labels), `aliases` (alternative note names for link suggestions), `cssclasses` (CSS classes for styling).

See [PROPERTIES.md](references/PROPERTIES.md) for all property types, tag syntax rules, and advanced usage.

## Tags

```markdown
#tag                    Inline tag
#nested/tag             Nested tag with hierarchy
```

Tags can contain letters, numbers (not first character), underscores, hyphens, and forward slashes. Tags can also be defined in frontmatter under the `tags` property.

## Comments

```markdown
This is visible %%but this is hidden%% text.

%%
This entire block is hidden in reading view.
%%
```

## Obsidian-Specific Formatting

```markdown
==Highlighted text==                   Highlight syntax
```

## Math (LaTeX)

```markdown
Inline: $e^{i\pi} + 1 = 0$

Block:
$$
\frac{a}{b} = c
$$
```

## Diagrams (Mermaid)

````markdown
```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Start] --> B{Decision}
    B -->|Yes| C[Do this]
    B -->|No| D[Do that]
```
````

To link Mermaid nodes to Obsidian notes, add `class NodeName internal-link;`.

## Footnotes

```markdown
Text with a footnote[^1].

[^1]: Footnote content.

Inline footnote.^[This is inline.]
```

# Agent Teams cho Doanh nghiệp

## Tổng quan

Agent Teams là mô hình triển khai nhiều **AI Agent** phối hợp làm việc cùng nhau, tự động hóa các quy trình phức tạp trong doanh nghiệp. Mỗi Agent đóng một vai trò chuyên biệt, giao tiếp và phân công nhiệm vụ theo workflow đã thiết kế.

## Kiến trúc cơ bản

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Orchestrator Agent] --> B[Research Agent]
    A --> C[Writing Agent]
    A --> D[Code Agent]
    A --> E[Review Agent]
    B --> |Dữ liệu thô| C
    C --> |Bản nháp| E
    E --> |Feedback| C
    E --> |Hoàn thiện| F[(Output)]
```

## Các vai trò chính trong Agent Team

- **Orchestrator** — Điều phối, phân việc, giám sát tiến độ
- **Researcher** — Tìm kiếm, thu thập và tổng hợp thông tin
- **Writer / Creator** — Soạn thảo nội dung, tài liệu, báo cáo
- **Coder / Developer** — Viết code, sửa bug, review PR
- **Reviewer / QA** — Kiểm tra chất lượng, validate output
- **Deployer** — Triển khai, vận hành, monitoring

## Lợi ích cho Doanh nghiệp

> [!tip] Tăng năng suất gấp nhiều lần
> Thay vì 1 nhân viên làm tuần tự, Agent Teams xử lý ==song song== nhiều công đoạn cùng lúc.

- **24/7 vận hành** — Không giới hạn giờ làm việc
- **Khả năng mở rộng** — Thêm Agent mới khi cần
- **Chất lượng đồng đều** — Review tự động, ít sai sót
- **Giảm chi phí** — Tối ưu nguồn lực nhân sự cho việc sáng tạo

## Công cụ & Nền tảng phổ biến

| Nền tảng | Đặc điểm | Phù hợp |
|---|---|---|
| Claude Code + Hermes | Multi-agent delegation, cron jobs, memory | SME, startup |
| CrewAI | Framework Python, role-based agents | Developer team |
| AutoGen (Microsoft) | Multi-agent conversation | Research, enterprise |
| LangGraph | Graph-based workflows | Complex pipelines |

## Ví dụ Workflow: Báo cáo Thị trường

> [!example] Quy trình 4 bước
> 1. **Research Agent** → Thu thập dữ liệu thị trường
> 2. **Analysis Agent** → Phân tích, xử lý số liệu
> 3. **Writing Agent** → Soạn báo cáo hoàn chỉnh
> 4. **Review Agent** → Kiểm tra, chỉnh sửa trước khi gửi

## Tham khảo

- [[AI Agent Overview]]
- [[Hermes Agent Setup]]
- [[Prompt Engineering Best Practices]]

---

> [!warning] Lưu ý triển khai

function.name: write_file
function.arguments:
{"content":"# Workflow Chi Tiết — Nhóm 1, 2, 3: Productivity, Customer Support, Content Marketing

> **Tổng:** 30 agents | Nhóm 1: Productivity (#1–#10) | Nhóm 2: Customer Support (#11–#20) | Nhóm 3: Content Marketing (#21–#30)
> **Bối cảnh:** Doanh nghiệp SME Việt Nam, 50–500 nhân sự, dùng SaaS phổ biến

---

## NHÓM 1 — PRODUCTIVITY & TASK AUTOMATION

---

### #1 Email Auto-Responder
> **Trigger:** Email mới đến inbox (Gmail/Outlook webhook)
> **Input:** Email gốc (subject, body, sender, thread history)
> **Output:** Draft phản hồi sẵn sàng gửi, hoặc tự gửi nếu tin cậy cao

**Workflow:**
1. **Phân loại email** — AI đọc subject + body, xác định loại: hỏi giá, hỗ trợ kỹ thuật, partnership, spam, nội bộ, khẩn cấp. Dựa vào lịch sử thread để hiểu ngữ cảnh.
2. **Trích xuất intent & context** — Lấy thông tin chính: khách hỏi gì, deadline nào, cần ai xử lý. Tra dữ liệu nội bộ (bảng giá, FAQ, CRM) để có câu trả lời chính xác.
3. **Soạn draft phản hồi** — Viết email reply theo đúng tone brand (lịch sự, chuyên nghiệp). Đính kèm link/file liên quan nếu cần. Nếu email phức tạp → tạo draft chờ duyệt; nếu đơn giản (cảm ơn, xác nhận) → tự gửi.
4. **Gắn flag & logging** — Đánh tag email, log vào CRM/Notion, set reminder follow-up nếu cần.

**Tools:** Gmail API / Microsoft Graph API, Claude/GPT-4, n8n/Zapier, Notion API, CRM (HubSpot)

---

### #2 Calendar Optimizer
> **Trigger:** Yêu cầu đặt lịch mới HOẶC mỗi sáng tự review lịch trong ngày
> **Input:** Danh sách cuộc họp, preference user (khung giờ focus, múi giờ), lịch team
> **Output:** Lịch tối ưu với time blocks (deep work, buffer, break)

**Workflow:**
1. **Scan lịch hiện tại** — Lấy toàn bộ event từ Google/Outlook Calendar 7 ngày tới. Đọc priority tags, attendee list, location.
2. **Phân tích & xếp hạng** — Gán trọng số: họp với sếp/khách = cao, internal sync = trung bình, optional = thấp. Xác định time-wasting meetings (không agenda, quá dài).
3. **Tối ưu rearrange** — Dời meeting linh hoạt vào khung phù hợp. Tạo deep-work blocks 2 tiếng không gián đoạn. Chèn buffer 15 phút giữa các cuộc họp. Đề xuất bỏ/giảm các meeting ít giá trị.
4. **Đề xuất & apply** — Gửi đề xuất cho user qua Slack/Telegram. Nếu user approve → tự cập nhật calendar. Gửi reminder trước mỗi cuộc họp.

**Tools:** Google Calendar API / Outlook Calendar API, Claude, n8n, Slack/Telegram webhook

---

### #3 Meeting Summarizer
> **Trigger:** Cuộc họp bắt đầu trên Zoom/Google Meet/Teams
> **Input:** Audio stream cuộc họp (real-time hoặc recording)
> **Output:** Bản tóm tắt + action items + decisions + transcript đầy đủ

**Workflow:**
1. **Ghi âm & transcribe** — Bật bot tham gia cuộc họp (Zoom OAuth / Meet recording). Dùng Whisper hoặc built-in transcription để chuyển audio → text theo thời gian thực. Phân biệt speaker bằng voice diarization.
2. **Tóm tắt inteligent** — AI phân tích transcript, tách thành: (a) Tóm tắt chính (3-5 bullet), (b) Decisions đã chốt, (c) Action items (ai làm gì, deadline), (d) Vấn đề chưa giải quyết.
3. **Format & phân phối** — Đưa tóm tắt vào template Notion/Google Docs. Gửi qua Slack channel hoặc email cho participants. Tự động tạo task trên Jira/Trello cho mỗi action item.

**Tools:** Zoom/Google Meet API, OpenAI Whisper, Claude, Notion API, Slack webhook, Trello/Jira API

---

### #4 Task Prioritizer
> **Trigger:** Task mới tạo HOẶC review định kỳ mỗi sáng 8:00
> **Input:** Danh sách tasks (từ Todoist/Jira/Notion), deadline, priority labels
> **Output:** Tasks đã xếp hạng theo urgency × importance matrix

**Workflow:**
1. **Thu thập tasks** — Pull tất cả tasks từ các nguồn: Todoist, Jira, Notion, email flagged, Slack starred. Merge và deduplicate.
2. **Đánh giá Eisenhower Matrix** — AI phân loại mỗi task theo 2 trục: Urgency (deadline gần, dependency blocker) và Importance (revenue impact, sếp yêu cầu, customer-facing). Kết quả: 4 góc phần tư.
3. **Xếp hạng & suggest** — Sắp xếp theo score tổng hợp. Đề xuất: nào làm ngay, nào delegate, nào schedule, nào drop. Nhắc task quá hạn hoặc sắp deadline.
4. **Cập nhật hệ thống** — Push ranking ngược lại Todoist/Notion. Gửi bản tóm tắt qua Telegram mỗi sáng.

**Tools:** Todoist API / Notion API / Jira API, Claude, n8n, Telegram webhook

---

### #5 Document Formatter
> **Trigger:** Document mới upload hoặc gửi qua email/Slack

### #41 Code Reviewer
> **Trigger:** Pull Request mới được mở

> **Output:** Review comments + approval recommendation + quality score

**Tools:** GitHub API / GitLab API (gh CLI), SonarQube, Claude, ESLint/Pylint, n8n

### #42 Bug Reproducer
> **Trigger:** Bug report mới tạo (Jira/GitHub Issue)
> **Input:** Bug description, steps to reproduce, expected vs actual behavior, environment info
> **Output:** Reproduction result (confirmed/unable to reproduce) + minimal reproduction case

**Workflow:**
1. **Parse bug report** — AI đọc description, extract: symptoms, error messages, affected component, environment (browser, OS, API version). Identify relevant code files.
2. **Analyze codebase** — Search code for relevant functions/modules. Trace code path from user action → bug location. Identify likely root cause areas.
3. **Attempt reproduction** — Write automated test case attempting to reproduce. Run in CI with matching environment. If reproduced → capture stack trace, logs, minimal reproduction steps. If not → add more edge case tests.
4. **Report back** — Update issue: "✅ Reproduced" with minimal steps + test case attached, or "❌ Unable to reproduce" with environment differences noted. Suggest fix direction if root cause identified.

**Tools:** GitHub API, Claude, pytest/Jest, Docker, CI/CD (GitHub Actions), Jira API

### #44 Test Case Generator

> **Input:** Source code (functions, classes, endpoints)

**Workflow:**

### #45 Dependency Updater

> **Output:** Updated dependencies + compatibility test results + changelog summary

4. **Create PR** — Bundle updates: "🔒 Security fixes (3 critical)" + "⬆️ Minor updates (12 packages)" + "⚠️ Major updates (2 packages, manual review needed)". Changelog summary for each major update.

**Tools:** Dependabot/Renovate, Snyk, npm audit, GitHub Actions, Claude, n8n

### #47 Log Analyzer
> **Trigger:** Khi error/alert triggered HOẶC scheduled analysis hàng ngày
> **Input:** Application logs (ELK Stack, CloudWatch, file logs)
> **Output:** Root cause analysis + correlated events + fix suggestions

**Workflow:**
1. **Collect relevant logs** — Pull logs từ time window quanh incident (±30 phút). Filter: ERROR, WARNING, exception stack traces. Include context logs (INFO level) trước/sau error.
2. **Pattern analysis** — AI tìm: error clusters (same error nhiều lần), correlated events (Service A fail → Service B timeout → Service C error), temporal patterns (happens daily at 2am?), deployment correlation (just deployed v2.1?).
3. **Root cause identification** — Trace error chain backward from symptom → proximate cause → root cause. Map to code location (file:line). Check: recent code changes in that area? Infrastructure change? Config change?
4. **Report & suggest** — "Root cause: Database connection pool exhausted (max_connections=100, current load=150). Fix suggestion: increase pool size OR optimize slow queries. Related: PR #234 merged yesterday added 3 new DB queries per request."

**Tools:** ELK Stack (Elasticsearch), CloudWatch Logs, Claude, Datadog, n8n, GitHub API

### #48 Code Refactoring Agent
> **Trigger:** Manual request HOẶC scheduled codebase health check
> **Input:** Source code files, quality metrics
> **Output:** Refactored code with explanations + quality improvement metrics

**Workflow:**
1. **Assess code health** — Run analysis: complexity (cyclomatic), duplication (DRY violations), code smells (god class, long method, feature envy), test coverage gaps. Identify top 10 refactoring candidates.
2. **Plan refactoring** — Prioritize by impact: high complexity + frequently changed files first. Choose technique: extract method, extract class, rename, introduce parameter object, replace conditional with polymorphism. Ensure tests exist before refactoring.
3. **Execute refactoring** — Apply changes incrementally. Run tests after each change. Maintain behavior (refactoring = no functionality change). Update related code and tests.
4. **Review & merge** — Create PR with before/after comparison: "Reduced cyclomatic complexity from 15 → 5. Eliminated 120 lines of duplication. Improved test coverage from 45% → 78%." Human review required.

**Tools:** SonarQube, Claude, GitHub API, pytest/Jest, n8n, complexity analyzers

### #50 Security Vulnerability Scanner

> **Output:** Vulnerability report + severity classification + fix recommendations

**Workflow:**
1. **Scan codebase** — Run: SAST (static analysis: Semgrep, CodeQL), dependency check (Snyk, Dependabot), secret detection (git-secrets, truffleHog), container scan (Trivy) if Docker used.
2. **Analyze findings** — AI triage: eliminate false positives. Classify: 🔴 Critical (RCE, SQL injection, auth bypass), 🟡 High (XSS, CSRF, info leak), 🟢 Medium/Low (misconfig, best practice). Map to OWASP Top 10.
3. **Generate remediation** — For each finding: explain vulnerability, provide specific code fix, estimate exploit difficulty, assess business impact. Prioritize: internet-facing + critical severity first.
4. **Track & verify** — Create issues/tickets for each vulnerability. Assign to team. Verify fixes with re-scan. SLA: Critical = 24h, High = 7 days, Medium = 30 days. Monthly security posture report.

**Tools:** Snyk, Semgrep, Trivy, OWASP ZAP, GitHub Advanced Security, Claude, Jira API, n8n

### #72 Budget Forecaster

> **Input:** Historical spend data, planned activities, growth assumptions
> **Output:** Budget forecast 3-12 tháng + variance analysis

**Workflow:**

3. **Scenario planning** — Best case / base case / worst case. What-if: "Nếu revenue giảm 20%, cắt chi phí đâu?" "Nếu mở thêm chi nhánh, cần thêm bao nhiêu budget?"

### #73 Fraud Detector
> **Trigger:** Mỗi giao dịch mới + daily batch review
> **Input:** Transaction data (amount, merchant, time, location, patterns)
> **Output:** Risk score + flagged transactions + investigation report

**Workflow:**
1. **Real-time screening** — Mỗi transaction: check amount (bất thường vs history?), location (khác usual?), frequency (nhiều transaction nhỏ liên tiếp?), merchant category (high-risk?), time (3AM transaction?).
2. **Pattern matching** — AI detect known fraud patterns: round amounts, just-below-threshold, split transactions, new payee + large amount, duplicate payments, ghost employee patterns, vendor shell company indicators.
3. **Score & action** — Risk score 0-100. >80: block transaction + immediate alert finance manager. 50-80: flag for review within 24h. <50: pass through. Log all decisions for audit trail.
4. **Investigation support** — For flagged items: compile evidence (similar past transactions, related entities, timeline). Generate investigation summary. Track resolution: confirmed fraud / false positive / under investigation.

**Tools:** Python/scikit-learn, Claude, ERP/banking API, n8n, Telegram/Slack alert, Google Sheets

### #75 Tax Compliance Checker

### #76 Accounts Payable Automator

### #77 Revenue Reconciliation Agent
> **Trigger:** Hàng ngày (batch) + end of month full reconciliation
> **Input:** Bank statements, payment gateway records, invoices, ERP entries
> **Output:** Matched records + unmatched items + reconciliation report

**Workflow:**

### #86 Risk Assessment Agent

4. **Mitigation plan** — For each Critical/Significant: recommend mitigation (contract clause, insurance, operational control, contingency plan). Cost of mitigation vs cost of risk. Final recommendation: proceed/modify/stop.

### #90 Whistleblower Triage Agent

4. **Acknowledge & track** — Send acknowledgment to reporter (if channel allows). Create confidential case file. Track investigation progress. Reporter can check status anonymously. Retention per policy after resolution.

### #91 Inventory Forecaster
> **Trigger:** Hàng tuần + trước mùa cao điểm
> **Input:** Historical sales, current inventory, lead times, seasonality
> **Output:** Demand forecast + reorder recommendations + safety stock levels

**Workflow:**
1. **Data collection** — Pull: 12-24 tháng sales history theo SKU, current stock levels, pending orders, supplier lead times, seasonal patterns (Tết, back-to-school, year-end).
2. **Forecast demand** — Time series model (Prophet/ARIMA) per SKU. Factor: trend, seasonality, promotions planned, market growth. Generate demand forecast 4-12 weeks ahead per SKU.
3. **Calculate reorder points** — For each SKU: ROP = (daily demand × lead time) + safety stock. Consider: MOQ (minimum order quantity) từ supplier, quantity discounts, storage constraints. Flag: items at risk of stockout, items at risk of overstock.
4. **Recommendations** — Purchase order suggestions: what to order, how much, when, from which supplier. Prioritize: stockout-risk items first. Cash flow impact of recommended orders. Push to procurement team.

**Tools:** Python/Prophet, ERP/Shopify API, Claude, Google Sheets, Metabase, n8n

---

### #92 Supply Chain Monitor
> **Trigger:** Continuous (daily batch) + alert on exceptions
> **Input:** Shipment tracking data, supplier updates, logistics data
> **Output:** Shipment status dashboard + delay alerts + impact assessment

**Workflow:**
1. **Track shipments** — Pull tracking data from: shipping APIs (GHTK, Viettel Post, Ninja Van), freight forwarders, supplier dispatch confirmations. Map all in-transit items: origin, destination, ETA, current status.
2. **Detect exceptions** — Flag: delays (>2 days behind schedule), stuck shipments (no update >48h), customs hold, damaged reported, wrong address. Predict cascade impact: "Shipment A delayed → production line B stops in 3 days."
3. **Alert & escalate** — Auto-notify relevant teams: procurement (supplier delay), warehouse (reschedule receiving), sales (customer impact). Severity-based escalation: critical → manager call, standard → Slack notification.
4. **Post-incident analysis** — Monthly: on-time delivery rate, average delay, top delay reasons, worst-performing routes/suppliers. Feed data into vendor evaluation (#94).

**Tools:** Shipping APIs (GHTK, Viettel Post), Claude, n8n, Metabase/Grafana, Slack/Telegram alert

### #93 Route Optimizer
> **Trigger:** Daily dispatch (morning) + ad-hoc rush orders
> **Input:** Delivery orders, vehicle fleet, driver locations, traffic data
> **Output:** Optimized routes per vehicle + estimated times + fuel cost

**Workflow:**
1. **Collect orders** — Pull today's deliveries: addresses, time windows (before 10am, 2-5pm), priority (express/standard), package size/weight. Geocode all addresses.
2. **Optimize routing** — Vehicle routing problem (VRP) solver: minimize total distance/time while respecting: capacity per vehicle, time windows, driver shifts, priority orders first. Consider: real-time traffic (Google Maps/OSRM), road restrictions.
3. **Assign & dispatch** — Allocate routes to drivers: sequence of stops, optimal path on map, estimated time per stop. Send via driver app or Telegram. Include: customer name, phone, notes, COD amount.
4. **Monitor & adjust** — Real-time tracking: driver completed stop X, behind schedule? → re-optimize remaining route. Handle: failed delivery (re-schedule), urgent add-on (insert into best route). End-of-day performance report.

**Tools:** Google Maps/OSRM API, OR-Tools (VRP solver), Claude, n8n, Telegram, driver tracking app

### #94 Vendor Evaluator
> **Trigger:** Hàng quý + khi contract renewal approaching
> **Input:** Vendor performance data, pricing, quality metrics, delivery records
> **Output:** Vendor scorecard + ranking + renewal/negotiation recommendations

**Workflow:**
1. **Collect data** — Pull from ERP: purchase history, delivery performance (on-time rate), quality (defect rate, return rate), pricing (vs market), responsiveness (issue resolution time). Survey internal stakeholders.
2. **Score vendors** — Weighted scoring: Quality (30%) + Delivery (25%) + Price (20%) + Service (15%) + Compliance (10%). AI adjust weights per category importance. Historical trend: improving or declining?
3. **Benchmark** — Compare: vendor pricing vs market average, quality vs alternatives, total cost of ownership (including hidden costs: delays, defects, management overhead). Identify: over-reliance on single vendor.
4. **Recommendations** — Tier: Preferred (auto-renew, better terms), Approved (continue, negotiate), Probation (improvement plan needed), Replace (find alternative). Suggest negotiation talking points per vendor.

**Tools:** ERP API, Claude, Google Sheets, Notion API, n8n, survey tool (Typeform)

---

### #95 Warehouse Slotting Agent
> **Trigger:** Quarterly optimization + when layout changes needed
> **Input:** SKU data (dimensions, velocity, pick frequency), warehouse layout
> **Output:** Optimal slotting plan + pick path optimization

**Workflow:**
1. **Analyze SKU velocity** — Classify: A-items (top 20% by pick frequency → golden zone), B-items (middle → secondary zone), C-items (slow movers → back/top). Analyze: seasonal velocity changes, co-picking patterns (items often ordered together).
2. **Map warehouse** — Current layout: zones, racks, bins, aisles. Measure: pick travel time per order, congestion points, empty/wasted space. Identify: fast movers in back, slow movers in prime locations.
3. **Optimize layout** — AI generate slotting plan: A-items at ergonomic height, near dispatch. Co-picked items adjacent. Heavy items low. FIFO compliance for dated items. Minimize total pick travel distance.
4. **Implementation plan** — Phased move plan (don't disrupt operations). New bin assignments per SKU. Updated warehouse management system. Expected improvement: X% faster picking, Y% less travel distance. Schedule review after 30 days.

**Tools:** WMS API, Claude, Python (optimization), Google Sheets, n8n, floor plan tools

---

### #96 Demand Planning Agent
> **Trigger:** Hàng tháng + khi có event bất thường
> **Input:** Sales history, market data, promotions calendar, economic indicators
> **Output:** Demand plan by SKU/region/channel + confidence intervals

**Workflow:**
1. **Multi-source data** — Historical sales (by SKU, region, channel). External: holiday calendar, competitor activities, weather forecast, economic indicators (GDP, consumer spending). Internal: planned promotions, new product launches, price changes.
2. **Build forecast** — Ensemble model: statistical (Prophet) + ML (gradient boosting) + AI judgment (Claude analyzes qualitative factors). Generate demand per SKU × region × week for next 12 weeks.
3. **Scenario analysis** — Base case + upside (promotion works well) + downside (economic slowdown). Sensitivity: "Nếu tăng giá 10%, demand giảm bao nhiêu?" Risk-adjusted demand plan.
4. **Consensus & publish** — Present to S&OP meeting. Compare vs sales team forecast. Reconcile differences. Publish agreed demand plan → drives procurement (#91), production scheduling, logistics planning. Track forecast accuracy monthly.

**Tools:** Python/Prophet/scikit-learn, Claude, ERP API, Google Sheets, Metabase, n8n

---

### #97 Quality Control Inspector
> **Trigger:** Each production batch / delivery received + weekly analysis
> **Input:** QC data (measurements, test results, defect logs), specifications
> **Output:** Pass/Fail + defect analysis + root cause + corrective actions

**Workflow:**
1. **Collect QC data** — Pull from: measurement devices, visual inspection records, lab test results, customer complaint data. Compare against specifications: dimensions, weight, appearance, performance, packaging.
2. **Statistical analysis** — Calculate: defect rate per batch, control charts (SPC), Cp/Cpk (process capability). Identify: trends (deteriorating?), patterns (specific shift/line/operator?), outliers.
3. **Root cause** — For failures: fishbone analysis (man, machine, material, method, environment). AI suggest likely causes based on pattern: "Defect type X thường do nguyên nhân Y trong lịch sử." Link to specific batch, supplier, operator.
4. **Corrective actions** — Recommend: "Supplier A material batch #123 → tăng inspection. Machine B cần calibration. Operator C cần training." Track: corrective action implementation, effectiveness verification. Report weekly quality dashboard.

**Tools:** Python/SciPy (SPC), Claude, ERP/MES API, Google Sheets, Metabase, n8n

---

### #98 Fleet Management Agent
> **Trigger:** Continuous monitoring + maintenance schedule
> **Input:** Vehicle data (GPS, odometer, fuel, maintenance history, driver logs)
> **Output:** Fleet status + maintenance schedule + cost optimization

**Workflow:**
1. **Monitor fleet** — Real-time GPS tracking: location, speed, idle time. OBD data: fuel consumption, engine health, tire pressure, mileage. Driver behavior: harsh braking, speeding, excessive idle.
2. **Maintenance scheduling** — Predictive maintenance: "Vehicle A đã 45,000km, lịch thay dầu 50,000km → schedule tuần tới." Based on: manufacturer schedule + actual usage + AI wear prediction. Avoid scheduling during peak delivery.
3. **Cost optimization** — Track: fuel cost per km (compare vehicles, identify inefficient), maintenance cost trending, total cost of ownership. Recommend: replace vehicles when maintenance cost exceeds threshold. Insurance optimization.
4. **Compliance** — Track: registration renewal dates, insurance expiry, driver license renewal, inspection deadlines. Alert 30 days before each. Ensure logbook compliance. Driver hours tracking.

**Tools:** GPS/OBD API, Claude, Google Sheets, n8n, Metabase, Telegram alert, calendar API

---

### #99 Returns Processor
> **Trigger:** Customer initiates return request
> **Input:** Return request (order ID, reason, photos), order data, return policy
> **Output:** RMA authorization + return shipping + refund/exchange processing

**Workflow:**
1. **Validate return** — Check: order exists, within return window (7/14/30 days per policy), reason valid (defective, wrong item, change of mind). Verify with photos if required. Check: customer return history (serial returner?).
2. **Authorize RMA** — If valid → generate RMA number, create return shipping label (GHTK/Viettel Post). Send customer: instructions + label + timeline. If invalid → reject with explanation.
3. **Process return** — When item received: QC inspection (matches description? damaged in transit?). If OK → process refund (original payment method) or exchange (ship replacement). If not OK → partial refund or reject, with evidence.
4. **Analytics** — Track: return rate by product, reason, supplier. Flag: high-return products for quality review. Identify: return fraud patterns. Feed data into product improvement and supplier evaluation.

**Tools:** Shopify/WooCommerce API, payment gateway API, shipping API, Claude, n8n, ERP API

---

### #100 Sustainability Reporter
> **Trigger:** Hàng quý + annual ESG report
> **Input:** Energy consumption, waste data, emissions, supply chain sustainability data
> **Output:** Sustainability report + metrics dashboard + improvement recommendations

**Workflow:**
1. **Collect data** — Pull: electricity bills, fuel consumption, water usage, waste volumes (by type), paper usage, fleet emissions. Supplier sustainability data (if available). Employee commuting survey data.
2. **Calculate metrics** — Carbon footprint: Scope 1 (direct emissions - fleet, generators), Scope 2 (electricity), Scope 3 (supply chain estimates). Per-unit metrics: carbon per product, energy per revenue. Waste diversion rate. Water intensity.
3. **Benchmark** — Compare vs: previous periods (improving?), industry averages, regulatory targets (VN commitments: Net Zero 2050), GRI Standards compliance, customer requirements.
4. **Report & recommendations** — Quarterly sustainability brief. Annual ESG section for annual report. Top 5 reduction opportunities: "Switch fleet to EV saves X tons CO2/year." Cost-benefit of each initiative. Set targets for next period.

**Tools:** Utility APIs, Claude, Python/Pandas, GRI Standards template, n8n, Google Sheets, Metabase

{"success": true, "name": "obsidian", "description": "Read, search, create, and edit notes in the Obsidian vault.", "tags": [], "related_skills": [], "content": "---
name: obsidian
description: Read, search, create, and edit notes in the Obsidian vault.
---

# Obsidian Vault

Use this skill for filesystem-first Obsidian vault work: reading notes, listing notes, searching note files, creating notes, appending content, and adding wikilinks.

## Related sub-skills (load for specialized tasks)

These are installed under the `obsidian` skill category and provide deep Obsidian-specific knowledge:

- **`obsidian-markdown`** — Full Obsidian Flavored Markdown syntax: wikilinks, embeds, callouts, properties, comments, highlights. Load when creating/editing `.md` files with Obsidian-specific features. Has references: CALLOUTS.md, PROPERTIES.md, EMBEDS.md.
- **`obsidian-bases`** — Obsidian Bases (`.base` files): database views with filters, formulas, summaries (table/cards/list/map). Load when creating `.base` files or the user mentions Bases. Has reference: FUNCTIONS_REFERENCE.md.
- **`defuddle`** — Extract clean markdown from web pages via `defuddle parse <url> --md`. Prefer over generic web fetch for articles/docs. CLI installed globally (v0.18.1+).

## Vault path

Use a known or resolved vault path before calling file tools.

The documented vault-path convention is the `OBSIDIAN_VAULT_PATH` environment variable, for example from `~/.hermes/.env`. If it is unset, use `~/Documents/Obsidian Vault`.

File tools do not expand shell variables. Do not pass paths containing `$OBSIDIAN_VAULT_PATH` to `read_file`, `write_file`, `patch`, or `search_files`; resolve the vault path first and pass a concrete absolute path. Vault paths may contain spaces, which is another reason to prefer file tools over shell commands.

If the vault path is unknown, `terminal` is acceptable for resolving `OBSIDIAN_VAULT_PATH` or checking whether the fallback path exists. Once the path is known, switch back to file tools.

## Read a note

Use `read_file` with the resolved absolute path to the note. Prefer this over `cat` because it provides line numbers and pagination.

## List notes

Use `search_files` with `target: "files"` and the resolved vault path. Prefer this over `find` or `ls`.

- To list all markdown notes, use `pattern: "*.md"` under the vault path.
- To list a subfolder, search under that subfolder's absolute path.

## Search

Use `search_files` for both filename and content searches. Prefer this over `grep`, `find`, or `ls`.

- For filenames, use `search_files` with `target: "files"` and a filename `pattern`.
- For note contents, use `search_files` with `target: "content"`, the content regex as `pattern`, and `file_glob: "*.md"` when you want to restrict matches to markdown notes.

## Create a note

Use `write_file` with the resolved absolute path and the full markdown content. Prefer this over shell heredocs or `echo` because it avoids shell quoting issues and returns structured results.

## Append to a note

Prefer a native file-tool workflow when it is not awkward:

- Read the target note with `read_file`.
- Use `patch` for an anchored append when there is stable context, such as adding a section after an existing heading or appending before a known trailing block.
- Use `write_file` when rewriting the whole note is clearer than constructing a fragile patch.

For an anchored append with `patch`, replace the anchor with the anchor plus the new content.

For a simple append with no stable context, `terminal` is acceptable if it is the clearest safe option.

## Targeted edits
